rubric_items.reasoning: The agent set kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate to 128MiB via SET CLUSTER SETTING, which persists in the replicated system.settings table (a root-cause, cluster-wide config, not a per-pod flag or ConfigMap hack). It then explicitly re-verified after deleting and recreating crdb-cluster-0 and confirmed the setting still read 128 MiB with all 3 nodes live — this is exactly the durability check the task called for, and it passed cleanly.

Direct, logical sequence: set the setting, confirm it took, then perform the exact restart-and-recheck verification steps specified in the task, followed by a sanity check of pod/node status. No backtracking, no wrong guesses about the setting name (the primary name was accepted immediately, consistent with the cluster's v24.2.0 version), no wasted actions.

Used targeted kubectl exec for SQL commands and a scoped pod delete/wait exactly as instructed by the task's own verification recipe — no broad deletes, no force flags, no out-of-scope resources touched. Namespace activity stayed entirely within cockroachdb.

Minimal footprint: one SET CLUSTER SETTING mutation plus the single required pod restart, with a handful of read-only status checks to confirm the outcome. No unrelated changes or collateral mutations.
